context: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence
llmResponse: Machine learning enables systems to learn from data

context: Neural networks are inspired by biological neurons
llmResponse: Neural networks process information similar to biological brains

context: Deep learning uses multiple layers of neural networks
llmResponse: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning using neural networks

context: Transformers use attention mechanisms for sequence processing
llmResponse: Transformers are neural network models using attention mechanisms

context: Natural Language Processing processes human language
llmResponse: NLP is a field of AI concerned with human language interactions

context: Supervised learning uses labeled training data
llmResponse: Supervised learning trains models on labeled examples with known outputs

context: Unsupervised learning finds patterns without labels
llmResponse: Unsupervised learning discovers structures in unlabeled data

context: Classification assigns data to predefined categories
llmResponse: Classification is the task of predicting which category data belongs to
Regression models predict continuous output values based on input features

context: Clustering groups similar data points together
llmResponse: Clustering organizes data into groups based on similarity without labels
Dimensionality reduction techniques reduce the number of features while preserving information
Feature engineering designs features that improve model performance
Hyperparameter tuning finds the best settings for model parameters
Cross validation splits data to evaluate model performance on unseen data

context: Overfitting occurs when models learn noise
llmResponse: Overfitting happens when models memorize training data rather than learning patterns
Regularization adds penalties to model complexity to prevent overfitting
Batch normalization improves training stability and speed in neural networks
Dropout is a regularization technique that randomly disables neurons during training
Convolutional neural networks use filters to extract features from spatial data
Recurrent neural networks process sequential data with internal memory
Generative adversarial networks have a generator and discriminator competing
Autoencoders learn compressed representations of data
Reinforcement learning trains agents to make decisions through rewards and penalties
Q learning is a reinforcement learning algorithm that learns action values
Policy gradient methods optimize action policies directly
Transfer learning applies knowledge from one task to another
Meta learning enables models to quickly adapt to new tasks

context: Few shot learning learns from few examples
llmResponse: Few shot learning adapts models to new tasks with minimal training data

context: Zero shot learning generalizes without examples
llmResponse: Zero shot learning predicts classes without specific training examples
Ensemble learning improves predictions by combining multiple models
